---
title: "LRPDID07base: Waitlist-crossover (DiD) pooled session-dose response on letter-sound knowledge (L)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
did_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "did_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPDID07base is the **pooled-dose comparator** to LRPDID07: the same
waitlist-crossover / difference-in-differences session-dose response on
letter-sound knowledge (YARC-LSK, `yarclet`), but with a **single pooled** dose
slope `beta_dose` instead of partial-pooled per-period slopes. It stacks the two
early periods (P1 = t1->t2, P2 = t2->t3) for both arms; each child is their **own
control** (a child random intercept), and the immediate arm - treated in both
periods - anchors the time/maturation trend.

The pair (LRPDID07 period-varying vs LRPDID07base pooled) is nested on the same
outcome and rows, so the PSIS-LOO comparison between them tests whether the
letter-sound dose-gain slope varies by period (#135).

::: {.callout-note title="What this estimand is, and is not"}

The per-period session count is the **exposure** (`IS`): this is the **ID-3
observational dose-response**, **confounded by GA -> IS**, and must be read as an
*adjusted association* - never "more sessions cause more gain". The DiD machinery
adjusts only `{IG, A}` and **never conditions on cumulative sessions**
(`attend_cumul`, the `IS` collider). Only the randomised contrast (the ITT
LRPITT07, or the DiD arm term LRPDID02) is causal.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Sign convention and the ceiling"}

Positive => the intervention helps. The Beta-Binomial **logit** link absorbs
ceiling effects: an immediate-arm child near the top of a bounded test makes a
small P2 gain *expected*, not a spurious negative time trend - which is exactly the
failure mode of a raw-gain DiD on the count scale.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_{i,p} = \alpha + \beta_{\text{period}}\,[p = \text{P2}]
           + \beta_{\text{dose}}\,z(\text{sessions}_{i,p})
           + \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}_*(L_{\mathrm{pre},i,p}, N)
           + \gamma_A\,A_{\text{std},i,p} + u_{\text{child},i},
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood on the period post-count, $N = 32$. `sessions` is
the standardised per-period intervention-session count. Priors follow the shared
template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.png){width=32%}
![tau](prior_tau.png){width=32%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.png){width=32%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.png){width=32%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.png){width=32%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Crossover / DiD dose-response summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
did_summary
```

- `beta_dose_*`: the pooled session-dose slope on the logit scale (effect per 1 SD
  of intervention sessions; mean, equal-tailed central interval at coverage
  `ci_prob`).
- `beta_period_*`: the period (time/maturation) anchor from the immediate arm.
- `prob_*_pos`: posterior probability the coefficient is positive.

Read `beta_dose` as an **adjusted association**, not a causal dose effect (see the
estimand note). The period-resolved counterpart and the nested LOO test live in
LRPDID07 and the cross-model comparison (`did_dose_loo_compare.csv`).

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
